meaning: n.守卫,警戒,护卫队,防护装置vt.保卫,看守,当心vi.防止,警惕,警卫
examples: A guard was posted outside the building.
You can't let your guard down.
He was upgraded to security guard.
The castle was a bulwark, standing guard against various opposing armies and raiding parties for centuries.
The trouble is, small companies often do business with other small fries who jealously guard their financials.
